name: Volume
description: A collision volume system that triggers game events when players enter trigger zones. Supports kill volumes, win/lose conditions, damage zones, blocking volumes, and custom effects. Integrates with game state (lives, score, time) and broadcasts volume activation events. Features collision cooldown, physics removal, and preset configurations.
documentation:
## Volume
Create invisible zones that trigger game events on player collision.

### Presets
- **Blocking** - Blocks characters, enemies, and throwables
- **Kill** - Instantly kills the player (9999 damage)
- **Dialogue** - Triggers dialogue events
- **Loose** - Deducts points and time
- **Win** - Triggers win condition
- **Custom** - Configure all options manually

### Volume Options
- **Block Enemies/Throwables/Characters** - What the volume blocks
- **Damage Amount** - Damage dealt on contact
- **Loose Points/Time** - Points and time penalties

### Tips
- Use **Start On Trigger** for conditional activation
- Place invisible volumes as death zones below platforms
- Combine Kill preset with respawn for fall-off-map mechanics
author: stemstudio

startOnTrigger.name: Start On Trigger
startOnTrigger.description: When enabled, the volume only becomes active when triggered by a Trigger behavior instead of being always active.

blockEnemies.name: Block Enemies
blockEnemies.description: When enabled, enemies cannot pass through this volume and will be blocked by its collision.

blockThrowables.name: Block Throwables
blockThrowables.description: When enabled, throwable objects are blocked by this volume and cannot pass through it.

blockCharacters.name: Block Characters
blockCharacters.description: When enabled, player characters are blocked by this volume and cannot pass through it.

damageAmount.name: Damage Amount
damageAmount.description: Amount of damage dealt to the player on contact with this volume. Set to 9999 for instant kill.

loosePoints.name: Loose Points
loosePoints.description: Number of score points deducted from the player when they enter this volume.

looseTime.name: Loose Time
looseTime.description: Number of seconds deducted from the game timer when the player enters this volume.
